Activity Summary: Staging VM storage exhaustion and service recovery
Work Performed: VM root disk exhausted causing app/DB instability. PostgreSQL WAL (pg_wal) had grown significantly (~110GB). Backup/snapshot retention further consumed local disk. Stale dashboard pods remained; dashboard ran incorrect image tag (ccccdb4 vs required 2ec191d). Manual deploy initially failed due to placeholder image repo. Keycloak showed repeated CLIENT_LOGIN_ERROR for api-client service-account auth.
Outcome / Resolution: Validated infra with df/kubectl; identified WAL growth under Rancher/k3s storage; confirmed CNPG WAL archiving active but retained history filled disk; took VM snapshot; offloaded backups to object storage; cleaned old snapshots; expanded root disk and rebooted; cleaned stale pods; redeployed dashboard to iisc/arogyam-dashboard:2ec191d; reviewed Keycloak logs. Services restored; Keycloak client auth needs follow-up.
